examples.title: fork() System Call Example
examples.description: Demonstrates process creation using fork() system call
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates fork() which creates a child process. The parent gets the child's PID (>0), the child gets 0, and errors get -1. Both processes execute the same code after fork().

examples.title: File Operations using System Calls
examples.description: Demonstrates open(), read(), write(), and close() system calls
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates four essential file system calls: open() creates/opens file and returns file descriptor, write() writes data, read() reads data, close() closes the file descriptor.

examples.title: Inter-Process Communication using Pipe
examples.description: Demonstrates pipe() system call for parent-child communication
examples.code:
import os
import sys

# Create pipe using os.pipe() - wrapper for pipe() system call
read_fd, write_fd = os.pipe()

# Fork the process
pid = os.fork()

if pid < 0:
    print("Fork failed!")
    sys.exit(1)
elif pid == 0:
    # Child process
    # Close read end - child will only write
    os.close(read_fd)
    
    # Write message to pipe
    message = "Hello from child process!"
    os.write(write_fd, message.encode())
    
    # Close write end
    os.close(write_fd)
    sys.exit(0)
else:
    # Parent process
    # Close write end - parent will only read
    os.close(write_fd)
    
    # Wait for child
    os.wait()
    
    # Read from pipe
    data = os.read(read_fd, 100)
    print(f"Parent received: {data.decode()}")
    
    # Close read end
    os.close(read_fd)
examples.explanation: pipe() creates a unidirectional communication channel. os.fork() creates child process. Parent writes through write_fd, child reads through read_fd. This demonstrates IPC using pipes.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is message size

examples.title: execlp() System Call Example
examples.description: Demonstrates exec() family to replace process image with new program
examples.explanation: execlp() is part of exec() family. It replaces the current process image with a new program. Here the child process executes 'ls -la' command. If exec succeeds, no code after it runs in child.
